commonRules: Preserve only the product, brand, audience, platform, and tone cues that are explicitly present in the source brief.
Treat missing details as open constraints instead of inventing brand facts or user needs.
Use inspiration references to extract transferable design logic rather than copying a single layout, visual treatment, or narrative beat literally.
outputRequirements: Return a reusable design contract that can drive design.md, implementation-plan artifacts, and a Canvas-ready prototype path.
Keep the direction premium, specific, and implementable instead of generic or template-driven.

formats.label: Premium editorial landing page
formats.lead: Study the inspiration references and synthesize a premium editorial landing page system that translates the source brief into a reusable, brand-specific direction.
guardrails: Prioritize one dominant hero idea and a clear narrative progression instead of dashboard-style clutter.
Favor a full-bleed visual approach with restrained supporting copy and deliberate section sequencing.
deliverables: Translate the references into a reusable design contract that fits the product story, audience, and brand voice in the source brief.

formats.label: B2B dashboard or app shell
formats.lead: Use the inspiration references and source brief to extract a reusable dashboard and app-shell contract that stays fast, calm, and operator-friendly.
guardrails: Favor predictable layout zones, strong state communication, and clean action paths over decorative UI noise.
Keep the interaction model credible and scalable across desktop breakpoints.
deliverables: Return a dashboard contract that can guide a first implementation plan and a Canvas-first prototype handoff.

formats.label: Cinematic product-story or photostudio direction
formats.lead: Study the inspiration references and synthesize a cinematic product-story direction that turns the source brief into a premium, product-led narrative.
guardrails: Do not fall back to generic ecommerce grids, trust-badge clutter, or spec-dump layouts.
Prioritize product-story clarity over commerce-system conventions and keep the scene count disciplined.
deliverables: Return a premium product-led story with 3 to 5 clear scene beats and a strong visual hierarchy.

formats.label: Mobile-first onboarding and activation flow
formats.lead: Work from the inspiration references and source brief to define a mobile-first onboarding and activation experience that moves a user from curiosity to a confident first action.
focusAreas: visual rules that should carry into the core product
guardrails: Keep the system simple, emotionally clear, and conversion-focused instead of overloaded with feature marketing.
Make the outcome feel native, modern, and brand-specific rather than template-driven.
deliverables: Return a reusable onboarding direction that can become both a contract and an implementation-ready prototype plan.

formats.label: Existing product redesign from inspiration
bestFor: quality upgrades without a full rebrand
formats.lead: Compare the source brief against the inspiration references and synthesize a redesign contract that upgrades the product without discarding its core identity.
guardrails: Avoid superficial mimicry and avoid proposing a totally unrelated brand system.
Favor the smallest coherent visual shift that creates a real quality jump.
deliverables: Return a transformation plan that is specific enough to drive implementation-plan artifacts and clear enough to hand directly into Canvas.
